title: Federal Schedule I status of marijuana
text: Marijuana remains Schedule I under the federal Controlled Substances Act. Interstate transport across state lines = federal felony regardless of state legality. No federal medical recognition (separate from FDA cannabidiol). Status drives banking + tax + research restrictions.

title: §280E deduction prohibition for controlled substances
text: No deduction or credit allowed for ordinary + necessary business expenses paid in carrying on any trade or business that consists of trafficking in Schedule I/II controlled substances. Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) is allowed (CHAMP doctrine + 471 regulations). Marketing, rent for non-production areas, payroll for non-production employees = disallowed. Audited heavily by IRS.

title: METRC tracking + tag system
text: State-mandated track-and-trace for all cannabis plants + immature plants + harvest lots + packages + transfers. RFID tag at plant level. Daily inventory reconciliation required; variance reportable per state rule (typ. ≤ 24-48 hr). Manifest required for every transfer between licensed premises. Sales must decrement package quantity in real time.

title: Cannabis transport manifest
text: Driver agent badge ID, origin + destination license numbers, manifest number, item list with METRC package tags, departure/arrival timestamps. Vehicle: locked, GPS-logged, between licensed premises only. No retail sale en route. Sample test transport allowed only between testing lab + licensed premises.

title: Certificate of Analysis (COA)
text: Required panels (state-specific): cannabinoid potency, terpenes (often), pesticides (per state list), residual solvents (post-extraction), heavy metals (Pb, Cd, As, Hg), microbial impurities + mycotoxins, foreign material, water activity / moisture. COA must be ≤ 12 months old typically. Label THC potency must be within state-allowed variance (typ. ±10%) of COA.

title: Child-resistant packaging
text: ASTM D3475 standard classification. PPPA (Poison Prevention Packaging Act) certification via 16 CFR 1700 panel test (50 children + 100 senior adults). State cannabis rules require CRP for any product containing cannabinoid. Re-sealable multi-serving units must remain CRP through life-of-package.

title: Youth-appealing marketing prohibitions
text: Prohibited: cartoons, designs targeting minors, statements/images of cartoon characters, candy-resembling shapes (gummy bears, sour candies in colors mimicking commercial brands), branding deceptively similar to children's products. Universal cannabis symbol required on product + package.

title: FinCEN 2014 marijuana banking guidance
text: Banks may serve cannabis businesses if implementing enhanced due diligence + filing marijuana-specific SARs (Marijuana Limited, Marijuana Priority, Marijuana Termination). Cole Memo enforcement priorities incorporated. CTR threshold $10,000 still applies. Structuring violations + SAFE Banking Act (pending) interaction.

title: COGS allocation for cannabis producers vs. resellers
text: Producers (cultivators + manufacturers) may capitalize labor + overhead into COGS under §471. Pure resellers (dispensaries) are restricted to invoice cost + transportation under §471(c). IRS LB&I has issued Industry Practice Unit on §280E. Document expense allocation methodology.

title: Buffer-zone requirements
text: Most states require minimum distance between cannabis retail and sensitive uses (K-12 schools, daycare, parks, churches). Typical 600 ft (CO) to 1000 ft (some local). Measured by straight-line property line to property line (varies). Pre-existing legal nonconforming use may be grandfathered. Always check local zoning overlay.
